senses.definition: the period of being an adult, characterized by physical and psychological maturity

examples.x: She felt a sense of freedom when she reached adulthood and could make her own choices.
Navigating the challenges of adulthood can be difficult, especially when balancing work and personal life.
The transition from childhood to adulthood often involves gaining independence and taking on new roles in society.

senses.definition: a period in one's life between puberty and adulthood

examples.x: Adolescence is a time of rapid physical growth.
She faced many challenges during her adolescence.
During adolescence, people often search for their identity.
His adolescence was marked by intense curiosity and learning.
Adolescence can be a confusing period full of self-discovery.

senses.definition: a young person who is in the process of becoming an adult

examples.x: The adolescent struggled to fit in with their peers.
Many adolescents face pressure to perform well academically.
The film was focused on the challenges faced by an adolescent.
The school offered counseling services for adolescents.
Adolescents often experience strong emotions as they grow.

senses.definition: a very young child, typically from birth to around one year old

examples.x: The pediatrician provided guidance to the new parents on caring for their infant's health and development.
During the flight, the mother cradled her sleeping infant in her arms, ensuring their comfort.
The infant's infectious laughter brought joy to everyone in the room.
The daycare center provides a safe and nurturing environment for infants while their parents are at work.

senses.definition: the period or state of very early childhood

examples.x: During infancy, babies develop rapidly, learning to crawl, babble, and explore their surroundings.
Parents play a crucial role in providing care and nurturing during their child's infancy, laying the foundation for healthy development.
The memories of infancy are often hazy, but some people claim to recall snippets of their early experiences.

senses.definition: the period or time of being a child, characterized by significant physical and emotional growth

examples.x: Sarah cherished the memories of her childhood spent playing in the backyard with her siblings.
Tom's childhood was filled with adventure and exploration as he roamed the forests near his home.
Emily's love for reading began in her childhood, when she would lose herself in books for hours on end.

senses.definition: the period of a male's life before he reaches adulthood

examples.x: During his boyhood, summers were spent exploring the woods behind his house, building forts, and catching fireflies.
He dreamt of becoming an astronaut ever since his boyhood fascination with the stars.
The worn baseball cap held memories of countless games played during his boyhood.

senses.definition: the period of a female individual's life before she reaches adulthood

examples.x: Sarah cherished the memories of her girlhood spent playing with dolls and dressing up in princess costumes.
Emily's love for reading blossomed during her girlhood, as she devoured every book she could get her hands on.
John's sister reminisced about the adventures they had shared during their carefree girlhood days.
The bond formed during their girlhood remained strong as Sarah and Emily navigated the challenges of adulthood together.

senses.definition: the later stage of life during which a person is considered old

examples.x: Sarah's grandmother remained active and independent well into her old age, inspiring those around her with her vitality.
Emily's love and care for her parents deepened as they entered old age, recognizing the importance of cherishing time together.
The retirement community offered a range of services and activities to enhance the quality of life for residents in their old age.

senses.definition: the period during someone's life when they stop working often due to reaching a certain age

examples.x: She looks forward to traveling during her retirement.
Retirement gives people the opportunity to pursue hobbies and relax.
Her retirement was filled with volunteer work and community activities.
Many people save for retirement using special savings accounts.
Retirement allowed him to spend more time with his grandchildren.

senses.definition: to get older

examples.x: As we age, our bodies undergo natural changes, including changes in skin elasticity and muscle tone.
Pets also age, and their care requirements may change as they become older.

senses.definition: advanced in age

examples.x: The elderly couple enjoyed taking leisurely walks together in the park.
The elderly man relied on a cane to aid his mobility.
The elderly residents of the nursing home appreciated visits from volunteers who provided companionship.
The elderly woman shared stories from her youth, reminiscing about times gone by.
The elderly gentleman greeted everyone with a warm smile and a twinkle in his eye.

senses.definition: related to parents or  the role of parenting

examples.x: The parental responsibility of providing for children's needs is paramount.
They attended a parental workshop to learn effective parenting techniques.
The parental bond between mother and child is often strong and enduring.
The school implemented a parental involvement program to engage parents in their children's education.
She sought parental advice from her own parents when facing difficult decisions.

senses.definition: brought to this world through birth
the time or period of one's life when they are not young anymore and are not old yet

examples.x: He started a new career during middle age.
Middle age is often a time for focusing on personal growth.
In middle age, she developed a passion for gardening.
Some people feel more confident in middle age than in their youth.
Middle age is sometimes called the “sandwich generation” phase.

senses.definition: the period during which a person is not old enough but is not young either
the state and quality of being mentally and behaviorally rational and sensible

examples.x: Tom admired his grandfather for his wisdom and maturity, gained through a lifetime of experiences.
Emily's parents encouraged her to demonstrate maturity by taking responsibility for her actions and decisions.
Mary's artwork displayed a level of maturity beyond her years, drawing praise from critics and art enthusiasts alike.

senses.definition: an infant very recently born

examples.x: The hospital provided classes to help new parents learn how to care for their newborns.

senses.definition: a young child who is starting to learn how to walk

examples.x: The toddler took his first steps, much to the delight of his parents.
She read a picture book to the toddler during storytime.
The daycare center had a special play area designed for toddlers.
The toddler’s first words were eagerly celebrated by the whole family.
They took the toddler to the park, where he enjoyed playing on the swings.

senses.definition: someone between the ages of 13 and 19

examples.x: Sarah's sister is a teen, currently navigating the challenges of high school.

senses.definition: a child who is between the ages of 9 and 12

examples.x: Sarah's daughter is a preteen, showing signs of independence and maturity as she approaches adolescence.
Mary's preteen cousin enjoys reading books and playing sports, exploring different interests as she grows older.
Emily's younger sister is a preteen, enjoying activities that bridge the gap between childhood and teenage years.

senses.definition: the period of one's life between childhood and adulthood

examples.x: Sarah cherished the memories of her youth, filled with laughter, friendships, and new experiences.
Tom's parents encouraged him to make the most of his youth, emphasizing the importance of education and personal growth.
Emily's passion for activism was ignited during her youth, as she became increasingly aware of social issues and injustices.
John's grandfather often reminisced about his youth, sharing stories of adventures and lessons learned along the way.

senses.definition: a person who is advanced in age, particularly one who qualifies for discounts, services, etc. provided for elderly people

examples.x: John's parents are seniors and benefit from discounted healthcare services.

senses.definition: intended for or related to young people, particularly in sports

examples.x: The junior division of the tennis tournament is open to players under the age of 18.
She coaches the junior soccer team, helping young athletes develop their skills and teamwork.
The junior league baseball game will be held on Saturday morning for players aged 12 to 14.
The junior swim meet attracts young swimmers from across the region to compete in various events.

senses.definition: (used by children or when talking to them) an adult who is fully matured and responsible

examples.x: Sarah admired her parents as the ultimate grownups, capable of handling any challenge that came their way.
Emily looked forward to becoming a grownup so she could make her own decisions and pursue her dreams.
As a grownup, responsibility often outweighs the whimsical freedom of childhood.
Despite the challenges, embracing the role of a grownup can lead to personal growth and fulfillment.

senses.definition: related to individuals who are considered elderly

examples.x: The senior doctor at the hospital has decades of experience in treating patients and training medical students.
The senior discount at the restaurant applies to customers over the age of 65.
The senior member of the team provides guidance and mentorship to younger colleagues.
